sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, has been experiencing chronic back pain for a couple of years, which started after moving furniture one weekend.
The pain is a sharp stabbing pain in the back that radiates down to the right leg, even to the big toe.
He also experiences numbness and tingling in the right leg, especially in the foot.
He has difficulty getting comfortable, especially when sitting.
The pain is exacerbated by lumbar flexion.
The patient denies any numbness or tingling in the genital or rectal area, weakness, loss of bladder or bowel control, or any family members with spine conditions.
He is a former smoker who quit 20 years ago.
Past medical history: The patient has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past, but with temporary relief.
Surgery was also mentioned as an option, which he wants to avoid.
He has gained weight due to his limited mobility.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his day is affected by his back pain, which limits his activities, including playing golf, which he has recently taken up.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: On musculoskeletal back exam, there is no overlying erythema or ecchymosis.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
On neurological examination, there is decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The patient's MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; Radiculopathy; Chronic back pain.
Plan: I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid, specifically a corticosteroid injection into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
The patient will be monitored for response to the injection, and the interval between injections will be determined based on individual response.
The patient will not need to take any medication long-term.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to assess the effectiveness of the injection and to schedule the next one if necessary.
